top_weaknesses.issue: Long dense paragraphs under each H2 — the 'early signals' paragraph is ~170 words, walls on mobile and buries the point
top_weaknesses.fix: Split the 170-word paragraph at 'These are not subprime markets' and break the 110-word policy paragraph at 'Whatever you think of the design'

top_weaknesses.issue: Essay-scaffolding tells: 'The phrase is not hyperbole', 'The temptation is to look back and say...', 'The honest question is whether...'
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace with concrete openings — e.g., open the section with the $1.6M two-bedroom example instead of the definition

top_weaknesses.issue: Title 'Loans Good on Day One' is evocative but missing search/topical signal (mortgage / housing / 2028)
top_weaknesses.fix: Subtitle or H2 could include 'structurally impaired mortgages' or '2028 mortgage scenario' to anchor topic

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice register shifts from narrative framing to analyst-mode (PV of cash flows) to op-ed punditry
top_weaknesses.fix: Keep the narrative voice in the definition section — lead with the underwriter's-eye example, then drop the technical aside

consensus_summary: Sharp thesis with a quotable closing line and a coherent H2 spine that the H2-skimmer reconstructs cleanly. Main fixes: split two long paragraphs for mobile and trim a few essay-scaffolding tells in section openers.
